text: A “truss” is an assemblage of beams, bars, or rods typically arranged in a triangle or combination of triangles to form a rigid framework and used as a structural support in buildings.

# Vehicle Code
## Division 1. WORDS AND PHRASES DEFINED [100 - 681]
##### § 657

A “truss” is an assemblage of beams, bars, or rods typically arranged in a triangle or combination of triangles to form a rigid framework and used as a structural support in buildings.

*(Added by Stats. 1979, Ch. 299.)*

subsections.text: A “truss” is an assemblage of beams, bars, or rods typically arranged in a triangle or combination of triangles to form a rigid framework and used as a structural support in buildings.
